figures.caption: Figure 2: Detailed architecture of MDSVM-UNet. (a) The overall encoder-decoder network with UNet++-style dense skip connections. The encoder comprises four MDSConv blocks and one RVM block, while the decoder consists of three RVM blocks and a convolutional output layer. (b) The Residual Visual Mamba (RVM) layer with residual connection and scaling factor. (c) The Vision State Space Module (VSSM) with dual-branch parallel processing.
Table 2: Quantitative comparison of two-stage segmentation methods on the ImageCAS dataset. Bold values indicate the best performance in each column. “–” indicates experiments not conducted.

Table 1: Quantitative comparison of single-stage segmentation methods on the ImageCAS dataset. Bold values indicate the best performance in each column. ↑ indicates higher is better; ↓ indicates lower is better.

Figure 1: Overview of the proposed two-stage coronary artery segmentation framework. In Stage 1, the input CTA volume is downsampled and segmented using MDSVM-UNet to produce a coarse segmentation map. The coarse results guide the extraction of 64× 64× 64 voxel blocks from the original resolution. In Stage 2, MDSVM-UNet performs fine-grained block-level segmentation, and the results from all blocks are merged to produce the final output.
